outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Trump says Iran deal is 'largely negotiated' — Axios exclusive reveals 60-day truce framework, nuclear issue deferred
outlets.summary: Washington Post reports Trump's Truth Social deal claim is backed by Axios exclusive revealing terms: 60-day truce, Hormuz opens toll-free, US lifts blockade, sanctions waivers for oil, nuclear file deferred. Netanyahu called deal 'very bad' after being excluded from negotiations.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Axios leaks US-Iran deal framework: Hormuz opens, nuclear talks deferred — but Senate hawks call it a 'disastrous mistake'
outlets.summary: CNN covers the Axios exclusive on deal terms alongside sharp congressional Republican opposition. Cruz called it a 'disastrous mistake,' Wicker said it would make 'everything accomplished by Operation Epic Fury for naught.'

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: US-Iran deal terms leaked: 60-day ceasefire, Hormuz reopening, sanctions relief — GOP hawks slam 'disaster'
outlets.summary: NPR summarizes the Axios exclusive alongside reaction: Pakistan PM Sharif congratulated Trump, Senate Republicans Wicker, Cruz, and Graham warned against the deal structure that defers nuclear issues. AAA: gas hits $4.56/gal Memorial Day high.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Top Republicans warn Trump against Iran deal: Cruz calls it 'disastrous mistake,' Wicker says 'finish the job'
outlets.summary: Fox News prominently covers Republican Senate opposition to the emerging Iran deal. SASC Chairman Wicker's formal statement — calling deal a 'disaster' that would render Operation Epic Fury 'for naught' — leads coverage alongside Cruz's 'disastrous mistake' warning.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: Axios reveals 60-day Iran deal terms — Senate hawks demand Trump 'finish the job' instead
outlets.summary: Washington Times frames the leaked deal terms (60-day truce, nuclear deferred) against hawkish Republican opposition from Wicker, Cruz, and Graham. Trump faces pressure from both sides: deal advocates and 'finish the job' bloc.

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: Wicker warning: 60-day Iran ceasefire would be 'ill-advised' disaster for Trump and US national security
outlets.summary: The Hill covers SASC Chairman Wicker's formal statement warning Trump against a 60-day ceasefire that defers nuclear issues. Wicker argues everything achieved by Operation Epic Fury would be reversed.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: US-Iran deal terms leak: Hormuz to reopen, nuclear talks deferred — Pakistan PM congratulates Trump
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera reports the Axios deal framework alongside Pakistan PM Sharif's congratulations to Trump. IRGC coordinated 35-ship Hormuz transit in 24 hours. Netanyahu excluded from talks, calls MOU 'very bad.' US blockade crosses 100-vessel milestone.

outlets.name: BBC News
outlets.headline: US-Iran deal closer as Axios reveals 60-day framework — but key disputes over uranium and Israel's exclusion remain
outlets.summary: BBC coverage: Axios leaked deal terms show 60-day structure with Hormuz opening and nuclear file deferred. Iran's Fars disputes Trump's 'largely negotiated' framing. Israeli exclusion and GOP opposition cloud potential announcement.

outlets.name: PBS NewsHour
outlets.headline: Inside the Iran deal framework: Hormuz reopens, bombs stop — but nuclear issue 'deferred' for 60 days
outlets.summary: PBS NewsHour analyzes the Axios framework: relief-for-performance structure means Hormuz opens first, then sanctions relief, then nuclear talks within 60 days. Iran's Araghchi called differences 'deep and significant.' AAA: gas $4.56/gal at Memorial Day.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Axios: 60-day Iran truce, Hormuz open, nuclear deferred — Netanyahu calls it 'very bad,' majority of Israelis oppose
outlets.summary: Times of Israel Day 86 liveblog: Axios leaks full deal terms. Netanyahu called deal 'very bad.' Survey shows majority of Israelis believe ending war under current conditions 'contradicts security interests.' Israel entirely excluded from negotiations by Witkoff and Trump.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Wicker, Cruz, Graham warn Trump against Iran deal — GOP hawks demand 'finish the job' as 60-day framework leaks
outlets.summary: Jerusalem Post highlights Senate Republican opposition to the emerging deal that aligns with Netanyahu's objections. Wicker, Cruz, and Graham all warned against a 60-day ceasefire that defers the nuclear file — same concern as Israel's security establishment.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Israel sidelined as Axios reveals US-Iran deal — Netanyahu furious, Witkoff pressing Trump not to resume war
outlets.summary: Haaretz analysis: Axios deal terms reveal Israel's core red lines (nuclear dismantlement, HEU export, proxy rollback) are all deferred or excluded. Witkoff reportedly pressing Trump not to resume war. Netanyahu cannot strike Iran unilaterally without US overflight.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran FM: Trump's 'largely negotiated' claim is 'incomplete' — IRGC coordinates 35-ship Hormuz transit
outlets.summary: Press TV: Iran's Fars news agency disputed Trump's 'largely negotiated' framing as 'incomplete and inconsistent with reality.' IRGC Navy coordinated 35-ship Hormuz transit in 24 hours, asserting sovereign control. FM Araghchi: enrichment is a non-negotiable red line.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Iran conveys response to US proposal through Pakistan — Araghchi: 'No enrichment means no deal'
outlets.summary: IRNA covers Iran's formal conveyance of its response to the US proposal via Pakistan mediator Gen. Munir. FM Araghchi reiterated Iran's red line: 'No enrichment would mean we do NOT have a deal.' Hormuz remains under Iran's IRGC management.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Ted Cruz: Iran deal would be 'disastrous mistake' — Tehran says IRGC's Hormuz control non-negotiable
outlets.summary: Iran International reports Cruz's 'disastrous mistake' warning prominently alongside Tehran's counter-framing that Hormuz will remain 'under Iran's management' and HEU will stay on Iranian soil regardless of any deal.
